backlog.title: v1.0 — Snapshot-Based AI Commit Message Generator
backlog.description: Ship Stagehand v1.0 per PRD §10.1: a single static Go binary that generates git commit messages by shelling out to the user's installed coding-agent CLI (pi, claude, gemini, opencode, codex, cursor). Implements the snapshot-based atomic commit flow (write-tree → generate → commit-tree → update-ref CAS), style learning with anti-duplicate guarantee, the provider-manifest system, full config precedence, CLI subcommands, and distribution via Homebrew/go install/releases. This phase covers all P0 and P1 functional requirements (FR1–FR51) and goals G1–G10.

milestones.title: Foundation Layer: Scaffold, Git Plumbing, and Configuration
milestones.description: Build the infrastructure layer with zero external product-logic dependencies: the Go module scaffold, the git wrapper that shells out to the real git binary for all plumbing/diff/log/stage operations, and the configuration system with full 7-layer precedence resolution. This milestone produces testable, isolated building blocks that all subsequent milestones consume.

tasks.title: Project Scaffold & Go Module Initialization
tasks.description: Create the Go module, directory structure per PRD §14, and build tooling so all subsequent work has a compilable, testable foundation.

subtasks.title: Initialize Go module, directory tree, and .gitignore
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/system_context.md §2 (project state = greenfield, only PRD.md exists). Go 1.26.4 installed; module targets go 1.22+ per PRD §22.3.
2. INPUT: No code exists. Start from the package layout in PRD §14 (h2.14).
3. LOGIC: Run `go mod init github.com/dustin/stagehand`. Create the full directory tree from §14: cmd/stagehand/, internal/{config,provider,prompt,git,generate,ui}/, pkg/stagehand/, providers/, docs/. Create a .gitignore excluding /bin/, *.test, coverage.out, dist/. Create a minimal go.mod (go 1.22). DO NOT add cobra/go-toml deps yet (that's later subtasks). Create empty .go files only where needed to make `go build ./...` pass (a main.go stub with `package main; func main(){}`).
4. OUTPUT: A compilable `go build ./...` with the full directory structure in place. go.mod with module path `github.com/dustin/stagehand`.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change yet.

subtasks.title: Create Makefile with build/test/lint/coverage targets
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §21.1 specifies `make build` → ./bin/stagehand, `make test`, `make lint`, `make coverage`. Version injected via `-ldflags "-X main.version=…"` at release (§21.1).
2. INPUT: Go module from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create a Makefile with targets: `build` (go build -o bin/stagehand ./cmd/stagehand), `test` (go test ./...), `lint` (golangci-lint run), `coverage` (go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... && go tool cover -func=coverage.out), `install` (go install ./cmd/stagehand), `clean` (rm -rf bin/ coverage.out dist/). Use `go test -race` in the test target. Add a `VERSION` variable defaulting to `dev` for the ldflags pattern.
4. OUTPUT: `make build` produces ./bin/stagehand; `make test` runs all tests; `make coverage` reports coverage.
5. DOCS: none — build tooling, no user-facing surface.

tasks.title: Git Wrapper Interface & Plumbing Commands
tasks.description: Implement the git wrapper that shells out to the real git binary (NO go-git dependency per PRD §22.3) for all plumbing commands: RevParseHEAD (with unborn detection), WriteTree, CommitTree, UpdateRefCAS, and DiffTree. This is the atomic-commit foundation from PRD §13.

subtasks.title: Git wrapper interface and command runner helper
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/git_plumbing_summary.md — always use `git -C <repo>` (not os.Chdir), capture stdout+stderr to separate buffers, pass args as []string (NEVER sh -c). See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §3 for the os/exec pattern.
2. INPUT: Go module from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/git/git.go defining a `Git` interface and a `gitRunner` struct that wraps exec.CommandContext for the real git binary. Provide a low-level `run(ctx, repo, args...) (stdout string, stderr string, exitCode int, err error)` helper that: resolves the git binary via exec.LookPath("git"), sets cmd.Dir via `-C <repo>`, captures stdout+stderr to bytes.Buffers, returns the exit code from *exec.ExitError. Define the interface methods: RevParseHEAD, WriteTree, CommitTree, UpdateRefCAS, DiffTree, StagedDiff, HasStagedChanges, RecentMessages, RecentSubjects, CommitCount, AddAll. Implement a constructor `New(workDir string) Git`. NO shell execution anywhere (PRD §19 security).
4. OUTPUT: internal/git/git.go with the Git interface and the run() helper. A real implementation struct that can be constructed and whose run() works against a temp git repo. Subsequent subtasks implement individual interface methods.
5. DOCS: none — internal package, no public surface. JSDoc/Go-doc comments on the interface are sufficient.

subtasks.title: RevParseHEAD with unborn-repo detection (exit-code check, NOT string emptiness)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: CRITICAL — see plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 1. `git rev-parse HEAD` on a repo with zero commits prints the literal string "HEAD" to stdout (NOT empty) and exits 128. The zsh commit-pi has a latent bug here. The Go port MUST check the exit code, not string emptiness.
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `RevParseHEAD(ctx) (sha string, isUnborn bool, err error)`. Run `git -C <repo> rev-parse HEAD`. If exit code == 128 → return ("", true, nil) meaning unborn/rootless repo. If exit 0 → return (trimmed stdout, false, nil). Any other error → return ("", false, err). Mock: the real git binary in a temp repo (unit test creates temp dir, git init, tests both born and unborn cases). No external services to mock beyond git itself.
4. OUTPUT: A method returning (sha, isUnborn, err). Downstream (CommitTree, UpdateRefCAS) uses isUnborn to decide root-commit handling.
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: WriteTree with merge-conflict failure detection
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/git_plumbing_summary.md. `git write-tree` serializes the current index into a tree object, prints the 40-char SHA, does NOT modify the index or HEAD (read-only w.r.t. refs). Fails with exit 128 if unresolved merge conflicts are in the index.
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `WriteTree(ctx) (treeSHA string, err error)`. Run `git -C <repo> write-tree`. On success return trimmed stdout (the TREE_SHA). On exit != 0, return a descriptive error mentioning 'unresolved merge conflicts' (detect 'merge' or 'needs merge' in stderr per git_plumbing_summary.md). Mock: real git binary in temp repo; test: success case (staged files → valid SHA), conflict case (create a conflict in the index, verify error).
4. OUTPUT: Returns TREE_SHA (immutable snapshot identifier) for consumption by CommitTree (P1.M1.T2.S4) and the rescue protocol (P1.M3.T3).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: CommitTree with stdin message delivery and root-commit handling
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 4. Use `-F -` (read message from stdin) to avoid ALL quoting issues with special characters, leading dashes, quotes, newlines. Go passes args as []string (no shell) so this is bulletproof. Root commit = omit `-p`. commit-tree does NOT move any ref (creates a dangling object).
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1. TREE_SHA from WriteTree (P1.M1.T2.S3). PARENT_SHA + isUnborn from RevParseHEAD (P1.M1.T2.S2).
3. LOGIC: Implement `CommitTree(ctx, treeSHA, parentSHA, message) (newSHA string, err error)`. Build args: `["-C", repo, "commit-tree", treeSHA]`. If parentSHA != "" (not unborn): append `-p`, parentSHA. Append `-F`, `-` (message via stdin). Set cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(message). On success return trimmed stdout (NEW_SHA). Mock: real git binary in temp repo; test root commit (no parent) and child commit (with parent).
4. OUTPUT: Returns NEW_SHA (the dangling commit object) for consumption by UpdateRefCAS (P1.M1.T2.S5).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: UpdateRefCAS (3-arg compare-and-swap, never force)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 3. CAS failure messages vary by version/scenario — do NOT substring-match. Treat exit != 0 as CAS failure. For root commit: expected-old = all-zeros hash (40 zeros for sha-1). NEVER use force-update (2-arg form) — PRD §13.2, §18.1.
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1. NEW_SHA from CommitTree (P1.M1.T2.S4). PARENT_SHA (expected-old) from RevParseHEAD (P1.M1.T2.S2).
3. LOGIC: Implement `UpdateRefCAS(ctx, newSHA, expectedOld) error`. Run `git -C <repo> update-ref HEAD <newSHA> <expectedOld>`. If expectedOld is the all-zeros hash (root commit / unborn), this CAS succeeds only if HEAD is truly unborn. On exit != 0 → return a typed error (e.g. ErrCASFailed) that the orchestrator can detect to print 'HEAD moved from <expected> to <actual>'. Mock: real git binary; test: success (correct expected-old), failure (stale expected-old → simulate by moving HEAD between capture and update).
4. OUTPUT: nil on success (HEAD atomically advanced); ErrCASFailed on mismatch. Consumed by the generate orchestrator (P1.M3.T4).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: DiffTree for 'what landed' reporting
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/git_plumbing_summary.md. `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status -r <sha>` prints tab-separated `<status>\t<path>` (A/M/D/R/C/T). For root commits, add `--root` to diff against the empty tree (otherwise no output).
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1. NEW_SHA from CommitTree (P1.M1.T2.S4). isUnborn/isRoot flag.
3. LOGIC: Implement `DiffTree(ctx, sha string, isRoot bool) ([]FileChange, error)` where FileChange{Status, Path, SrcPath}. Build args: `["-C", repo, "diff-tree", "--no-commit-id", "--name-status", "-r"]`. If isRoot: append `--root`. Append sha. Parse output: split each line on "\t"; 2 fields = status+path, 3 fields = status+src+dst (rename/copy). Mock: real git binary; test: child commit shows A/M/D, root commit with --root shows all as A.
4. OUTPUT: []FileChange for the success-report UX ('[<sha>] <subject>' + file list). Consumed by the CLI layer (P1.M4.T1).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

tasks.title: Git Diff Capture, Log Queries, and Staging
tasks.description: Implement the diff capture (with caps and exclusions matching commit-pi), the git log queries for style learning and duplicate detection, and the staging operations (auto-stage-all). These read-only and stage operations feed the prompt builder and the generate orchestrator.

subtasks.title: StagedDiff with markdown caps, exclusions, and byte truncation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 7 (no built-in --max-bytes; cap post-capture). See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/git_plumbing_summary.md §Diff Capture. The commit-pi script captures markdown diffs (≤100 lines/file) and non-markdown diffs (≤300KB, excluding lock/snap/map/vendor). Use pathspec `:!pattern` as separate argv elements (safe with exec.Command, no shell).
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1. Config: max_md_lines (default 100), max_diff_bytes (default 300000) from the config system (P1.M1.T4 — if not ready, use constants matching commit-pi defaults).
3. LOGIC: Implement `StagedDiff(ctx, opts DiffOpts) (string, error)` returning the concatenated payload. Part 1 (markdown): `git diff --cached --name-only -- '*.md' '*.markdown'` to get the file list, then for each file run `git diff --cached -- '<file>'` and cap at max_md_lines lines (split on \n, take first N). Part 2 (non-markdown): `git diff --cached -- ':!*.lock' ':!package-lock.json' ':!pnpm-lock.yaml' ':!yarn.lock' ':!*.snap' ':!*.map' ':!vendor/*' ':!*.md' ':!*.markdown'`, capture stdout, cap at max_diff_bytes bytes. If truncated, append a '\n... [diff truncated]' sentinel. Concatenate markdown section + non-markdown section. Mock: real git binary in temp repo with staged .md, .lock, .go files; assert caps and exclusions.
4. OUTPUT: A single diff payload string for consumption by the prompt builder (P1.M3.T1.S3) and the provider executor stdin (P1.M2.T5).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: HasStagedChanges (diff --cached --quiet exit-code semantics)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 6. `git diff --cached --quiet` exit 0 = nothing staged, exit 1 = staged changes exist, exit >1 = error. This is INVERTED from usual convention — must check exit code explicitly.
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `HasStagedChanges(ctx) (bool, error)`. Run `git -C <repo> diff --cached --quiet`. If exit 0 → return (false, nil). If exit 1 → return (true, nil). If exit >1 → return (false, err). Mock: real git binary; test: clean index (false), staged file (true).
4. OUTPUT: bool for the auto-stage-all decision (P1.M3.T2.S2) and the nothing-to-commit path (exit 2).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: CommitCount and RecentMessages for style learning
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 9. commit-pi uses `git log --format='---%n%B' -20` but `---` can collide with markdown horizontal rules in commit bodies. Use NUL-delimited: `git log --format='%x00%B' -20` and split on \x00 (cannot occur in text). Cap total at 100 lines.
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `CommitCount(ctx) (int, error)` running `git rev-list --count HEAD` (returns 0 on unborn). Implement `RecentMessages(ctx, n int, maxLines int) ([]string, error)` running `git log --format='%x00%B' -<n>`, split on NUL, trim, drop empty, cap total lines at maxLines (100). Mock: real git binary in temp repo with seeded commit history; test: single-line and multi-line commit detection.
4. OUTPUT: commit count (int) for mature-vs-new-repo prompt decision; []string of full commit messages for the prompt builder's style examples (P1.M3.T1.S1).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: RecentSubjects for duplicate detection
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD FR31 — fetch last 50 commit subjects via `git log --format=%s -50`. One subject per line. Used for exact-match duplicate check.
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `RecentSubjects(ctx, n int) ([]string, error)` running `git -C <repo> log --format=%s -<n>`. Parse output into a []string (one per line). Return empty slice on unborn repo. Mock: real git binary; test: returns N subjects, handles empty repo.
4. OUTPUT: []string of recent subjects for the dedupe check (P1.M3.T2). The dedupe logic builds a set/map from these for O(1) lookup.
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

subtasks.title: AddAll and staged file counting
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD FR16/FR20 — `git add -A` stages all changes. FR18 requires printing a notice 'Nothing staged — staging all changes (N files).' Need to count staged files after add. Count via `git diff --cached --name-only | wc -l` equivalent (split lines).
2. INPUT: The git runner helper from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `AddAll(ctx) error` running `git -C <repo> add -A`. Implement `StagedFileCount(ctx) (int, error)` running `git -C <repo> diff --cached --name-only` and counting non-empty lines. Mock: real git binary in temp repo; test: add -A stages modified+untracked, count is correct.
4. OUTPUT: AddAll stages everything; StagedFileCount returns N for the auto-stage notice. Consumed by the CLI layer (P1.M4.T1) and auto-stage logic (P1.M3.T2.S2).
5. DOCS: none — internal method.

tasks.title: Configuration System with 7-Layer Precedence
tasks.description: Implement the full configuration model per PRD §16: Config struct, built-in defaults, TOML file loading (global + repo-local via pelletier/go-toml/v2), git-config reader (stagehand.* keys), environment variables (STAGEHAND_*), and CLI flag precedence. The config feeds provider resolution and generation tuning.

subtasks.title: Config struct, defaults, and generation-tuning fields
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §2.2 for struct tagging with go-toml/v2. See §5.4 for the omitempty limitation (use pointers for nullable fields). PRD §16.1 lists the resolution order; §16.2 shows the config file structure.
2. INPUT: Go module from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/config/config.go defining: `Config` struct with fields: Provider (string), Model (string), Timeout (time.Duration), AutoStageAll (bool), Verbose (bool), NoColor (bool), MaxDiffBytes (int), MaxMdLines (int), MaxDuplicateRetries (int), SubjectTargetChars (int), Output (string: raw|json), StripCodeFence (bool). Create a `Defaults()` function returning Config with: Timeout=120s, AutoStageAll=true, MaxDiffBytes=300000, MaxMdLines=100, MaxDuplicateRetries=3, Output="raw", StripCodeFence=true, SubjectTargetChars=50 (per PRD §16.1). Use TOML tags matching §16.2 section names ([defaults], [generation]). Add `go-toml/v2` to go.mod.
4. OUTPUT: Config struct + Defaults() for consumption by all precedence layers (P1.M1.T4.S2-S4) and the provider registry (P1.M2.T3).
5. DOCS: none yet — public config docs come with the config init subtask (P1.M4.T1.S4, Mode A).

subtasks.title: TOML file loading (global + repo-local) with go-toml/v2
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §2.4 (overlay/merge pattern), §5.5 (decoding [provider.X] into map[string]Manifest). Global path: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/stagehand/config.toml (default ~/.config/stagehand/config.toml). Repo-local: ./.stagehand.toml. A missing file is NOT an error (return nil, nil). PRD §19 notes a repo-local config could redirect the provider — print a one-line notice when a repo-local config overrides the provider.
2. INPUT: Config struct + Defaults() from P1.M1.T4.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/config/file.go. Implement `loadTOML(path string) (*Config, error)` — read file, toml.Unmarshal into Config; if file doesn't exist return (nil, nil). Implement `globalConfigPath()` resolving XDG_CONFIG_HOME or ~/.config/stagehand/config.toml. Implement `overlay(dst, src *Config)` that merges non-zero fields from src into dst (field-by-field, NOT wholesale replace). For the Provider map (user-defined providers), merge key-by-key. Mock: temp TOML files; test: parse valid config, missing file (nil), merge partial override.
4. OUTPUT: A merged Config from file layers for the Load() orchestrator (P1.M1.T4.S4).
5. DOCS: none — internal. Config file format docs come with config init (P1.M4.T1.S4).

subtasks.title: Git-config reader (stagehand.* keys)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §2.5. Read `git config --get stagehand.<key>` for each known key (provider, model, timeout, auto_stage_all, etc.). Booleans via exit code or `git config --bool`. PRD §16.3 shows the git-config key structure.
2. INPUT: Config struct from P1.M1.T4.S1. The git binary (reuse internal/git runner or call git config directly).
3. LOGIC: Create a `loadGitConfig(repoDir) (*Config, error)` that reads stagehand.* keys via `git -C <repo> config --get stagehand.<key>` for each field. Parse timeout as duration (seconds → time.Duration). Parse booleans via `--bool` flag. Return a Config with only the fields that were found set. Missing keys are not errors (git config --get exits 1 for missing key). Mock: real git in temp repo with `git config stagehand.provider pi`; test: reads values, ignores missing.
4. OUTPUT: A partial Config (only set fields) for the precedence overlay (P1.M1.T4.S4).
5. DOCS: none — internal.

subtasks.title: Full precedence resolution (env vars + CLI flags) and Load() orchestrator
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §16.1 / FR34 precedence (highest→lowest): CLI flags > env vars (STAGEHAND_*) > repo git config > repo file (.stagehand.toml) > global file > built-in provider defaults > built-in defaults. See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §2.6-2.8. Env vars: STAGEHAND_PROVIDER, STAGEHAND_MODEL, STAGEHAND_TIMEOUT, STAGEHAND_CONFIG, STAGEHAND_VERBOSE, STAGEHAND_NO_COLOR (PRD FR35). For CLI flags, use pflag's flags.Changed() to detect explicitly-set flags (§2.7).
2. INPUT: Config struct (S1), TOML loader (S2), git-config loader (S3).
3. LOGIC: Create the `Load(ctx, opts LoadOpts) (*Config, error)` function in config.go that applies layers in precedence order: start with Defaults(); overlay global TOML; overlay repo TOML; overlay git config; overlay env vars (os.Getenv for STAGEHAND_*); overlay CLI flags (only flags.Changed() ones). For timeout, parse "120s" / integer seconds. Return the fully-resolved Config. LoadOpts includes: configPathOverride (from --config), repoDir, and a *pflag.FlagSet. Mock: table-driven tests with layered env/file/flag combinations asserting correct precedence.
4. OUTPUT: A single fully-resolved Config consumed by the CLI layer (P1.M4.T1) and the generate orchestrator (P1.M3.T4).
5. DOCS: none — internal. The config reference docs (env vars, precedence) are updated as Mode A in the relevant CLI subtask (P1.M4.T1.S1).

milestones.title: Provider System: Manifests, Rendering, Execution, and Parsing
milestones.description: Build the provider-manifest system (PRD §12) that normalizes the differences between six coding-agent CLIs. Includes the manifest schema, six compiled-in built-in manifests (verified against real --help output), the command-rendering algorithm, the subprocess executor with process-group management and timeout, and the robust output-parsing pipeline. This is the agent-agnosticism core.

tasks.title: Provider Manifest Schema and Merge Logic
tasks.description: Define the Manifest struct (PRD §12.1) that describes how to invoke one agent, with all fields for discovery, prompt delivery, print mode, model, system prompt, sub-provider, bare mode, output, retry, and environment. Implement the field-by-field merge logic for user overrides.

subtasks.title: Manifest struct with all §12.1 fields, TOML tags, and validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/external_deps.md for verified manifest field values. See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §5 (go-toml/v2 struct tags, no omitempty). All fields except name and command are optional with sensible defaults.
2. INPUT: Config struct from P1.M1.T4.S1 (manifests live in config under [provider.<name>]).
3. LOGIC: Create internal/provider/manifest.go defining `Manifest` struct with fields (TOML-tagged): Name, Detect, Command, Subcommand ([]string), PromptDelivery (stdin|positional|flag), PromptFlag, PrintFlag, ModelFlag, DefaultModel, SystemPromptFlag, ProviderFlag, DefaultProvider, BareFlags ([]string), Output (raw|json), JsonField, StripCodeFence (bool), RetryInstruction, Env (map[string]string). Add a `Validate() error` method ensuring Name and Command are non-empty, PromptDelivery is valid, Output is valid. Add a `DetectCommand()` that returns Detect or falls back to Command. Use pointer types or a merge approach for the omitempty limitation (§5.4). Define defaults: PromptDelivery="stdin", Output="raw", StripCodeFence=true, RetryInstruction=PRD §12.1 default.
4. OUTPUT: Manifest struct for the registry (P1.M2.T3), rendering (P1.M2.T4), executor (P1.M2.T5), and parser (P1.M2.T6).
5. DOCS: none — internal struct. Manifest field documentation comes with providers show (P1.M4.T1.S3, Mode A) and the reference manifest files (P1.M5.T2).

subtasks.title: Field-by-field manifest merge for user overrides
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §16.1 — 'Provider manifests merge field-by-field (a user override that sets only default_model leaves all other fields from the built-in manifest intact).' See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 5 and go_ecosystem_patterns.md §2.4 for the overlay pattern.
2. INPUT: Manifest struct from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `MergeManifest(base, override Manifest) Manifest` that overlays only non-zero/non-empty fields from override onto base. For slices (BareFlags, Subcommand), if override is non-empty, replace entirely. For Env map, merge key-by-key. This lets a user config with `[provider.pi] default_model = "glm-5.2"` override ONLY the model while keeping all pi bare_flags. Mock: unit tests merging partial overrides asserting untouched fields survive.
4. OUTPUT: A merged Manifest for the registry (P1.M2.T3).
5. DOCS: none — internal logic.

tasks.title: Built-in Provider Manifests (6 verified agents)
tasks.description: Compile in the six default provider manifests verified against real --help output. pi, claude, gemini, and opencode are first-class; codex and cursor include the discrepancy fixes identified during research.

subtasks.title: pi and claude manifests (explicit tool-disable switches)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. tasks.title: Provider Executor (Subprocess Run with Process Groups and Timeout)
tasks.description: Implement the executor that runs the rendered command: pipes stdin, captures stdout/stderr, enforces a context-based timeout, and kills the entire process group on cancellation. Includes the cross-platform process-kill abstraction (Unix Setpgid + Windows build tags).

subtasks.title: Subprocess executor (stdin pipe, stdout/stderr capture, env, timeout)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/go_ecosystem_patterns.md §3 (canonical os/exec pattern with cmd.Cancel + cmd.WaitDelay + SysProcAttr.Setpgid). PRD FR25 — configurable timeout (default 120s); on timeout kill agent and enter rescue. §18.4 — propagate SIGTERM to process group. Go 1.20+ cmd.Cancel/cmd.WaitDelay for clean context-based killing.
2. INPUT: CmdSpec from P1.M2.T4.S1 (Render). Config timeout from P1.M1.T4.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/provider/executor.go with `Execute(ctx, spec CmdSpec, timeout time.Duration) (stdout string, stderr string, err error)`. Build exec.CommandContext(ctx, spec.Command, spec.Args...). Set cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(spec.Stdin) if non-empty. Capture stdout+stderr to bytes.Buffers. Set cmd.Env = spec.Env. Apply process-group setup (delegate to S2's cross-platform abstraction). Set cmd.Cancel = kill process group with SIGTERM; cmd.WaitDelay = 3s grace before SIGKILL. Apply timeout via context.WithTimeout if timeout > 0. Return stdout, stderr, and err (context.DeadlineExceeded → timeout). Mock: a stub binary (e.g. /bin/cat or a tiny Go test helper) that echoes stdin to stdout; test: normal run, timeout kill, large stdin.
4. OUTPUT: stdout string (the agent's raw output) for the parser (P1.M2.T6). err signals timeout/failure for the orchestrator's retry/rescue.
5. DOCS: none — internal executor.

subtasks.title: Cross-platform process-group kill abstraction (Unix + Windows build tags)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 10. SysProcAttr.Setpgid is Unix-only. PRD §21.2 targets Linux/macOS/Windows × amd64/arm64. Windows needs CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP + GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent (or Job Objects). go_ecosystem_patterns.md §3.5 notes Setpgid isolates the child so terminal Ctrl-C doesn't reach it — parent must forward.
2. INPUT: Go module from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/provider/executil/ with build-tag-segregated files: exec_unix.go (//go:build !windows) providing `SetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd)` using SysProcAttr{Setpgid:true} and `KillProcessGroup(pid int, sig syscall.Signal)` using syscall.Kill(-pid, sig). exec_windows.go (//go:build windows) providing equivalents using CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP and GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent(CTRL_BREAK_EVENT, pid). A shared executil.go with the interface/func signatures. Mock: unit test on Linux (primary target); Windows path is structurally correct but tested in CI.
4. OUTPUT: SetProcessGroup() and KillProcessGroup() functions consumed by the executor (P1.M2.T5.S1) and signal handler (P1.M4.T2).
5. DOCS: none — internal abstraction.

tasks.title: Output Parsing Pipeline (Raw/JSON with Robust Fallback)
tasks.description: Implement the parseOutput pipeline (PRD §12.9) that extracts the commit message from the agent's raw stdout: trim, optional code-fence stripping, raw or JSON mode extraction with brace-balanced fallback, newline normalization.

subtasks.title: parseOutput pipeline (trim, fence strip, raw/json, JSON-in-prose, fallback)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §12.9 parseOutput pipeline: (1) TrimSpace; (2) if strip_code_fence and starts with ``` or ~~~, remove fence opener + everything after last closer, re-trim; (3) switch output: raw→msg=s, json→try json.Unmarshal whole, then brace-balanced substring, extract json_field; if fails, fall through to raw + set parse-fallback flag; (4) normalize \r\n→\n, collapse 3+ newlines to 2; (5) TrimSpace, ok = msg != "".
2. INPUT: Manifest struct (for Output mode, JsonField, StripCodeFence) from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/provider/parse.go with `ParseOutput(raw string, m Manifest) (msg string, ok bool, fellback bool)`. Implement the exact §12.9 5-step pipeline. For JSON mode: attempt json.Unmarshal on whole; on failure find first '{' and matching last '}' (brace-balanced count), retry. Extract m.JsonField from the map as string. On all JSON failures, treat cleaned stdout as raw message and set fellback=true. Code-fence stripping: detect ``` or ~~~ prefix, remove first line (opener + language tag) and from last closer onward. Mock: table-driven tests — raw clean, raw with fence, json valid, json-in-prose, json invalid→fallback, empty output→ok=false, multi-newline normalization.
4. OUTPUT: (msg, ok, fellback) for the orchestrator's retry decision (P1.M3.T4) and dedupe (P1.M3.T2). ok=false triggers a parse-retry with corrective instruction (FR29).
5. DOCS: none — internal parser.

tasks.title: Duplicate Rejection Loop

CONTRACT DEFINITION:

On exhausting retries: enter rescue path (FR33). commit-pi uses `grep -Fxq` for exact line match; Go uses a map/set for O(1) lookup.

The retry LOOP itself lives in the orchestrator (P1.M3.T4) which calls IsDuplicate and, on match, appends the subject to a rejected list and calls BuildUserPayload with the rejection list (P1.M3.T1.S3) for the next attempt. This subtask provides the check + extract functions; the orchestrator wires the loop. Mock: unit tests — duplicate detected, non-duplicate passes, subject extraction from multi-line message.

tasks.title: Rescue Protocol
tasks.description: Implement the rescue protocol (PRD §18.3 / FR43-FR45): when generation or parsing fails after the snapshot was taken, print the TREE_SHA, the exact manual recovery command, and optionally the candidate message.

subtasks.title: Rescue message formatting (TREE_SHA + recovery command + candidate message)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §18.3 / FR43-FR45. Rescue condition: TREE_SHA set and NEW_SHA not set. Print: failure notice, TREE_SHA, exact manual recovery command (`git commit-tree [-p <PARENT>] -m "…" <TREE> | xargs git update-ref HEAD`), omitting -p for root commit. If a candidate message was produced but rejected (dedupe/parse), print it too so the user's wait wasn't wasted (§18.3 last paragraph). Exit code 3.
2. INPUT: TREE_SHA + PARENT_SHA from the git plumbing (P1.M1.T2.S3, S2). Optional candidate message string.
3. LOGIC: Create internal/generate/rescue.go with `FormatRescue(treeSHA, parentSHA, candidateMsg string) string` returning the §18.3 formatted message. If parentSHA == "" (root commit): recovery command omits -p. If candidateMsg != "": append the 'A candidate message was produced but rejected' note with the message. The orchestrator (P1.M3.T4) calls this and the CLI layer prints it + exits 3. Mock: unit tests for rooted and rootless rescue messages.
4. OUTPUT: A formatted rescue string for the CLI layer to print (P1.M4.T1). Exit code 3 is set by the exit-code system (P1.M4.T3.S3).

subtasks.title: CommitStaged orchestrator: full pipeline (snapshot → generate → parse → dedupe → commit)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §11.3 — core is `commitStaged(ctx, cfg) error` that assumes the index is already staged. v1 main = maybeAutoStage(); commitStaged(). v2 main = for each partition { reset+stage; commitStaged() }. MUST NOT entangle staging with commit logic. §13 — the atomic flow: snapshot (write-tree) BEFORE generation; commit-tree creates dangling object; update-ref CAS is the only ref mutation. §11.1 data flow diagram. See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/system_context.md §3.
2. INPUT: All git plumbing (P1.M1.T2), diff/log (P1.M1.T3), provider executor+parser (P1.M2.T5/T6), prompt builders (P1.M3.T1), dedupe (P1.M3.T2), rescue (P1.M3.T3), stub provider (P1.M3.T4.S1).
3. LOGIC: Create internal/generate/generate.go with `CommitStaged(ctx, deps Deps, cfg Config) (Result, error)` where Result{CommitSHA, Subject, Message, Provider, Model}. Pipeline: (1) RevParseHEAD → (parentSHA, isUnborn); (2) StagedDiff → payload (if empty, return nothing-to-commit); (3) WriteTree → treeSHA (if fails, abort exit 1); (4) build system prompt (mature vs fallback based on CommitCount); (5) GENERATION+DEDUPE LOOP: for attempt in 0..maxRetries: BuildUserPayload(payload, rejectedList); Render manifest; Execute (with timeout); ParseOutput; if !ok → retry with retry_instruction (FR29); ExtractSubject; IsDuplicate? → append to rejectedList, continue; else break; (6) if still duplicate/unparseable after all retries → return rescue error (FormatRescue); (7) CommitTree(treeSHA, parentSHA, message) → newSHA; (8) UpdateRefCAS(newSHA, parentSHA-or-zero-hash) → if CAS fails, return CAS error (do NOT force); (9) DiffTree(newSHA, isUnborn) → file changes for report; (10) return Result. This function NEVER calls git add (staging is the CLI layer's job). Mock: integration tests using the stub provider (P1.M3.T4.S1) against a temp git repo — cover: success, dedupe-retry-then-success, parse-fail-rescue, CAS-failure (simulate by moving HEAD mid-test), root commit.
4. OUTPUT: Result{CommitSHA,...} or a typed error (ErrNothingToCommit, ErrRescue, ErrCASFailed, ErrTimeout) for the CLI layer (P1.M4.T1) and public API (P1.M3.T5).
5. DOCS: none — internal orchestrator. The public API docstring comes with P1.M3.T5.

tasks.title: Signal Handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM → Child Kill → Rescue)
tasks.description: Implement the signal handler (PRD §18.4 / FR45) that intercepts SIGINT/SIGTERM, forwards to the running child's process group, runs the rescue path if the snapshot was taken, and restores the default handler before the final update-ref.

subtasks.title: Signal handler: intercept, forward to process group, rescue, restore before commit
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/critical_findings.md FINDING 8 and go_ecosystem_patterns.md §4. Because Setpgid isolates the child, terminal Ctrl-C doesn't reach it — parent MUST forward via syscall.Kill(-pgid, sig). PRD §18.4: (1) if child running, SIGTERM it (then SIGKILL after grace); (2) if snapshot taken, run rescue; (3) restore default handler before final update-ref (matching commit-pi's `trap - INT TERM`). Use signal.Notify for SIGINT/SIGTERM.
2. INPUT: KillProcessGroup from P1.M2.T5.S2. FormatRescue from P1.M3.T3.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create a signal handler installed early in main (or in the generate flow). Use signal.Notify on a buffered channel for SIGINT/SIGTERM. On signal: if a child PID is registered (set by the executor after cmd.Start), forward the signal to the child's process group via KillProcessGroup. If the snapshot state (treeSHA) is non-empty, trigger the rescue path (FormatRescue + print + exit 3). Provide a mechanism to register/clear the current child PID and the snapshot state. Provide a `RestoreDefaultHandler()` called right before update-ref so a last-instant Ctrl-C isn't mistaken for failure. Mock: integration test — start a long-running stub provider, send SIGINT, verify child killed + rescue printed.
4. OUTPUT: A signal-handling module wired into the generate flow (P1.M3.T4) and CLI (P1.M4.T1).
5. DOCS: none — internal behavior. The rescue UX is documented via the rescue message itself.

subtasks.title: Property/invariant tests (idempotent index, atomic HEAD, snapshot immutability)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §20.2 — three invariants: (1) Idempotent index: after any failure path, `git diff --cached --name-only` is identical to before (no index mutation). (2) Atomic HEAD: after a CAS failure, `git rev-parse HEAD` is unchanged. (3) Snapshot immutability: `git cat-file -p <TREE_SHA>` is stable across the run regardless of subsequent staging. These are system-level safety assertions using the stub provider.
2. INPUT: CommitStaged (P1.M3.T4.S2) + stub provider (P1.M3.T4.S1).
3. LOGIC: Create integration test files that drive CommitStaged through failure paths (parse fail, timeout, CAS fail, SIGINT) and assert the three invariants via git queries before/after. Use a temp git repo. These tests prove the §18.1 invariant (repo byte-for-byte unchanged on failure). Mock: stub provider simulating each failure mode; real git binary.
4. OUTPUT: A property/invariant test suite that runs in CI and guards the repo-safety guarantee.
5. DOCS: none — test infrastructure.
